Package com.wip.controller

Class ProfileController

java.lang.Object
com.wip.controller.ProfileController
@Controller public class ProfileController extends Object
Spring MVC controller that manages the user profile view and edit flows.

This controller handles all routes under /profile, allowing authenticated users to view and update their customer profile information. It is role-aware: administrator accounts do not have an associated customer record, so they are directed to a dedicated admin-view page (profile/admin-view) instead of the standard customer profile view. Regular users can view their profile at /profile and edit it via the form at /profile/edit, which is submitted to /profile/update.

All mutations are guarded by Bean Validation on the CustomerDto, and service-layer exceptions are caught and surfaced back to the edit form via the errorMessage model attribute.

Since:
1.0
Version:
1.0
Author:
Rohith Varma K

  • Constructor Details

    • ProfileController

      public ProfileController(CustomerService customerService)
      Constructs a ProfileController with the required customer service.
      Parameters:
      customerService - service used to retrieve and update customer profile data

  • Method Details

    • viewProfile

      @GetMapping("/profile") public String viewProfile(org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Handles GET requests to /profile and renders the profile view page.

      The behaviour is role-dependent:

      • Admins are directed to profile/admin-view with the model attribute adminUsername set to the current username.
      • Regular users with a valid customer ID receive the customer model attribute populated from the database and are directed to profile/view.
      • If no customer ID can be resolved (unexpected state), the model attributes errorTitle and errorMessage are set and the view error/not-found is returned.
      Parameters:
      model - the Spring MVC Model used to pass profile data or error details to the view
      Returns:
      the logical view name: "profile/admin-view" for admins, "profile/view" for regular users, or "error/not-found" if the customer record cannot be resolved

    • editProfileForm

      @GetMapping("/profile/edit") public String editProfileForm(org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Handles GET requests to /profile/edit and renders the profile edit form.

      Admin users are redirected to /profile since they do not possess a customer record to edit. Regular users have their existing profile data pre-populated into the form via the customerDto model attribute so that current values are visible in the form fields.

      Parameters:
      model - the Spring MVC Model used to pre-populate the edit form with the existing CustomerDto data
      Returns:
      "redirect:/profile" for admins, "redirect:/login" if no customer ID can be resolved, or the logical view name "profile/edit" for regular users

    • updateProfile

      @PostMapping("/profile/update") public String updateProfile(@Valid @ModelAttribute("customerDto") @Valid CustomerDto customerDto, org.springframework.validation.BindingResult bindingResult, org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Handles POST requests to /profile/update and saves the edited profile data.

      The submitted CustomerDto is validated using Bean Validation constraints. If validation fails, the edit form is re-rendered with the binding errors. On success, the update is delegated to CustomerService.updateCustomer(Long, CustomerDto) and the user is redirected to /profile?updated so the view can display a success notification. Service-layer exceptions are caught and surfaced via the errorMessage model attribute without leaving the edit form.

      Parameters:
      customerDto - the form-backed DTO containing the updated profile fields; validated via @Valid
      bindingResult - holds any Bean Validation errors detected for customerDto
      model - the Spring MVC Model used to pass error messages back to the edit form on failure
      Returns:
      "redirect:/profile" for admins, "redirect:/login" if no customer ID can be resolved, "redirect:/profile?updated" on successful save, or "profile/edit" if validation or a service exception occurs
